unit_special_rules.Guts: Hero stat. Spend a point to shift any die roll by ±1 after it's rolled, to return an Order Die to the bag (reactivate a unit within 6"), or to cheat death (D6 + remaining Guts ≥ 7 keeps the Hero in play).
unit_special_rules.Luck: Hero stat. Spend a point to re-roll a die (one die per point). Can also give the Hero a reprieve when removed as a casualty.
unit_special_rules.Rift Mastery: Hero stat. Grants extra personal Rift Dice, usable for the Hero's own enhancements/weapons or allocated to nearby Rift units.
unit_special_rules.Flight: Can make powered leaps over terrain and enemies during Advance/Run moves, landing in Open Ground.
unit_special_rules.Fast: Moves further than ordinary infantry: 8" on an Advance, 16" on a Run.
unit_special_rules.Jump: A Vehicle may jump on Advance/Run Orders: pivot once at the start, then ignore all terrain while moving.
unit_special_rules.Heavy: Strong enough to wield squad-support weapons (LMG, Flamethrower) single-handed, ignoring their Team requirement.
unit_special_rules.Infiltrator: Scout/stealth troops. Gain an extra Cover-save bonus when they have no Advance/Run Order die; often deploy forward.
unit_special_rules.Tank Hunters: Close-assault anti-tank specialists. When winning an Assault against an Armoured Vehicle, damage is resolved as for a dedicated anti-tank weapon.
unit_special_rules.Tough Fighters: Roll one additional die for damage when fighting at Close Quarters.
unit_special_rules.Stubborn: Ignore negative Morale modifiers from Pin Markers when taking a Morale Check.
unit_special_rules.Fanatics: Ignore Pin-Marker Morale penalties (while at least two models remain); they fight rather than flee.
unit_special_rules.Amphibious: Can move forward over deep water at half speed with an Advance (no pivot or reverse while in the water).
unit_special_rules.Open-topped: Open turret/compartment: more exposed to HE and assaults than a fully enclosed vehicle.
unit_special_rules.Two-legged: A two-legged Walker may pivot up to twice (instead of once) when moving forward at Run speed.
unit_special_rules.Single-crew Walker: Operated by a single crew member, limiting how many of its weapons can be used at once.
unit_special_rules.Recce: Light recon vehicle: can reverse at its full Advance rate (e.g. when pulling back) rather than half.
unit_special_rules.Command Vehicle: Adds +1 Morale Value to itself and to friendly Vehicles within 12" (not cumulative with other bonuses).
unit_special_rules.Gun Shield: A shield protecting the crew, improving their save against fire from the front.
unit_special_rules.ATRDs: US disposable anti-tank rocket launchers (Anti-Tank Rocket Devices). Once per Order, one model may fire an ATRD instead of its normal weapon: 12", 1 shot, +6 Pen, Shaped Charge. Disposable (may fire all at once, then out of ammo).
unit_special_rules.Panzerfausts: Axis disposable anti-tank rockets. Once per Order, one model may fire a Panzerfaust instead of its normal weapon: 12", 1 shot, +6 Pen, Shaped Charge. Disposable (may fire all at once, then out of ammo).
unit_special_rules.Anti-tank Grenades: A squad upgrade giving the unit hand-thrown anti-tank grenades — the close-assault anti-tank gear (grenade bundles, sticky bombs and the like) that lets infantry threaten Armoured Vehicles in Close Quarters. It's the same category of weaponry the Tank Hunters rule represents; the core rulebook doesn't give it a separate stat line, so its effect is resolved through the assault / Close Quarters rules.
unit_special_rules.Engineers: Combat engineers: trained for demolitions and clearing obstacles/fortifications, and required to form an Engineer Platoon. The upgrade also unlocks certain equipment options (e.g. an infantry flamethrower) for the squad.
